title: ProviderModelContract
description: Language-agnostic contract for the provider/model registry shared across forgecode (Rust), OmniRoute (TypeScript), and cliproxy (Go). Version: 1.0.0. Each repo implements this contract in its native language; forgecode's forge_domain crate is the reference implementation.

ModelId.description: Unique identifier for a model within a provider, e.g. 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022' or 'gpt-4o'.

ProviderId.description: Unique identifier for a provider, e.g. 'anthropic', 'openai', 'open_router'. Built-in providers use snake_case.

InputModality.description: Input modality supported by a model. 'text' is universal; 'image' indicates vision capability.

Model.description: A single model offered by a provider.

description.description: Short description of the model's characteristics.

context_length.description: Maximum context window in tokens. Null when unknown.

tools_supported.description: Whether the model supports tool/function calling.

supports_parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether the model can invoke multiple tools in a single response turn.

supports_reasoning.description: Whether the model exposes a reasoning/thinking trace (e.g. Claude extended thinking, o1-series).

input_modalities.description: Input modalities accepted by the model. Defaults to ['text'] when omitted.

ProviderType.description: Category of the provider. 'llm' for chat completion providers (default); 'context_engine' for code indexing / search providers.

AuthKind.description: Authentication mechanism required by the provider. 'api_key': static bearer token. 'oauth2': device-flow or token-refresh required. 'aws_bedrock': AWS SigV4 signing. 'none': no auth (local/open providers).

ProviderConfig.description: Configuration for a single provider, merging built-in defaults with user overrides.

base_url.description: Base URL for the provider's API endpoint.

models.description: Explicit model list. If absent, the registry fetches the live model list from the provider's models endpoint.

env_key.description: Name of the environment variable that holds the API key for this provider, e.g. 'OPENAI_API_KEY'.

SseStopRule.description: Defines the terminal-marker rule set for SSE streams. Reference implementation: forge_eventsource::is_sse_terminal.

terminal_data_values.description: SSE event data field values that signal end-of-stream. '[DONE]' is the canonical OpenAI/Anthropic sentinel; '' (empty) is a keepalive/implicit-end marker.

openai_finish_reasons.description: Values of choices[0].finish_reason that indicate the model has finished generating.

anthropic_stop_fields.description: Anthropic SSE event fields that carry the stop reason.

synthetic_done_on_silent_close.description: When true, implementations must emit a synthetic [DONE] marker when the upstream connection closes without an explicit terminal event.

OAuthRefreshPolicy.description: Parameterized OAuth token refresh policy. The refresh_lead_seconds field is per-provider-overridable; the default 300 s matches all three repos (forgecode, OmniRoute, cliproxy for most providers). Providers with non-standard leads (e.g. codebuddy 86400 s) set their own override.

default_refresh_lead_seconds.description: Default number of seconds before token expiry at which a refresh should be triggered. Equivalent to forgecode `chrono::Duration::minutes(5)`, OmniRoute `TOKEN_EXPIRY_BUFFER = 5*60*1000`, cliproxy `5 * time.Minute`.

needs_refresh_semantics.description: Boolean predicate: a token needs refresh when the current time plus the lead window meets or exceeds the expiry timestamp.

description: Top-level contract document. A registry implementation MUST satisfy the constraints for Model and ProviderConfig; SSE and OAuth rules are normative for stream and auth layers respectively.

sse_stop_rule.description: Normative SSE terminal-marker rules for all stream implementations.
